_note: Batch D1 (ROUND 2, medieval Europe 800-1400 AD): documented parents + full siblings, going ONE MORE GENERATION BACK for round-1 spousal-parent additions in 40_spouseparents_d.json that had no ancestry shown. New ancestors use the R2D_ prefix. Unknown mothers/fathers are placeholders. Disputed/saga-only parentage flagged in-label. DEDUP: reused EXISTING ids ROLLO+POPPA (Adele of Normandy's parents), EDWARDELDER (Edmund I's father), and SPE_CONRADBURG+SPE_MATILDAFR (Gerberga of Burgundy's parents, merging into their existing union). Also cross-links two batch entries as kin: Rudolf II of Burgundy & Willa of Tuscany's mother Willa of Burgundy are full siblings (children of Rudolf I of Burgundy); Adelaide-Blanche of Anjou & Ermengarde-Gerberga of Anjou's father Geoffrey I Grisegonelle are full siblings (children of Fulk II of Anjou). SKIPPED (parentage genuinely undocumented/unresolved, no fabrication): SPE_ROBERTSTRONG (Robert the Strong -- no contemporary primary source; 3 conflicting modern theories, no scholarly consensus), SPK_DIETRICH_WEST & SPK_REINHILD (only a generic 'House of Widukind'/'Danish-Frisian' dynastic claim, no parents named by Wikipedia), SPH_WERNER5 (explicitly 'first documented Salian' -- no earlier ancestor known), SPJ_HEDWIGNORDGAU (parentage hypotheses exist but 'none backed by convincing evidence' per sources), SPJ_ERMENTRUDEGLEIBERG (no Wikipedia article; thin/conflicting genealogy-database sourcing, and her identity as Frederick of Luxembourg's wife is itself disputed), SPJ_ESTRIDOBOTRITE (father an unnamed Obotrite chief; 'no contemporary accounts naming specific relatives' per sources). Verified against Wikipedia/Asser/Heimskringla/Chronicle of the Kings of Alba, July 2026.
